time_pattern: Weekday morning and evening commute (40 minutes by metro)
travel_activity: Browse Xiaohongshu and Instagram to save street photography inspiration from Kyoto or Chiang Mai, organizing them into a 'Next City Walk' inspiration board
natural_expression: During my daily metro ride, I love scrolling through Xiaohongshu to see photos others have taken in Kyoto's alleys, saving them so I can find the same spots on my next trip.

travel_activity: Use Duolingo to learn basic Italian conversation or watch Italian dramas to review vocabulary, preparing linguistically for a future deep dive into Rome or Florence
natural_expression: Every Wednesday evening, I dedicate half an hour to learning Italian without fail. After all, next time I'm in Rome, I want to chat with the locals in the squares, not just rely on hand gestures.

time_pattern: The last weekend of every month
travel_activity: Organize photos taken during the month, color-grade and archive them using Lightroom, then select nine to post on WeChat Moments or write a short travel blog post
natural_expression: On the last weekend of the month, I export and edit the photos from my Sony A7C II, pick nine to post on WeChat Moments, and call it a wrap on the month's hustle.

travel_activity: Check flight price fluctuations, follow official accounts for special airfares, and compare prices for routes from Hangzhou to the Norwegian Fjords or Peru
natural_expression: Friday evenings are for relaxing by checking flight prices. I keep an eye on whether fares from Hangzhou to Norway or Peru have dropped, just to get a rough budget plan in place.

time_pattern: Two weeks before the Spring Festival holiday
travel_activity: Discuss itinerary details for the Qinghai-Tibet Railway with father Lin Jianguo, confirm train schedules and geographical highlights along the route, and plan next year's father-child travel route
natural_expression: With Chinese New Year approaching, I need to sync up with my dad, our family's 'living map,' on the Qinghai-Tibet Railway schedule. He's been talking about it for years, so next year I must take him to fulfill this dream.

time_pattern: End of each quarter (March, June, September, December)
travel_activity: Hold an online meeting with best friend Zhang Wei to leverage her Excel skills for price comparison, jointly planning the framework for the next long-haul international trip (e.g., New Zealand or Iceland)
natural_expression: At the end of every quarter, I have to pull Zhang Wei into a meeting; she handles the Excel price comparisons and itineraries while I dig up off-the-beaten-path attractions. That's the only way we can confidently tackle a trip to New Zealand or Iceland together.

time_pattern: Before the Plum Rain season in the Jiangnan region
travel_activity: Plan a weekend getaway within the Jiangnan region, seeking quiet ancient towns or seaside homestays to cope with upcoming work stress and anxiety
natural_expression: As soon as Hangzhou approaches the Plum Rain season, I rush to book a weekend trip somewhere in the Jiangnan area, finding a quiet ancient town or seaside spot to just zone out; otherwise, the work pressure becomes too much.

travel_activity: Plan a high-challenge trip (such as glacier hiking or skydiving) as a reward for completing major projects and to release energy
natural_expression: Every August on my work anniversary, if I've just wrapped up a major project, I want to fly to Iceland or Western Sichuan for some glacier hiking; I need that adrenaline rush to fully release my energy.

time_pattern: During the Singles' Day Shopping Festival
travel_activity: Purchase travel gear (such as new lenses, portable tripods) or stock up on common travel medications and comfortable bedding to prepare supplies for long-haul trips
natural_expression: During Singles' Day, I don't buy clothes; I just keep an eye on camera lenses and portable tripods. I also ask my mom to stock up on some cold medicine so I can feel secure filling my backpack with essentials for the next international trip.

travel_activity: Exchange tips with photography enthusiast 'Old K' regarding aurora shooting parameters and hidden spots in Iceland or Norway, updating personal shooting plans
natural_expression: Once the winter aurora season hits, I have to catch up with Old K to ask which hidden spots in Iceland currently have low light pollution and what settings are needed to capture that perfect green glow.

time_pattern: When feeling lost or hitting a career bottleneck
travel_activity: Reread journals and photos from the 2021 Iceland gap trip, or plan a visit to cultural cities like Rome or Kyoto to find direction through historical dialogue
natural_expression: Sometimes when I feel lost at work, I flip through my journal from that 2021 Iceland trip, or think about spending a few days in museums in Rome or Kyoto. Chatting with history always helps me find my direction.

time_pattern: Within one week after returning from any long-distance trip
travel_activity: Purchase and organize specialty fridge magnets and postcards from the trip; mail postcards to yourself or friends to complete the ritual of closing the travel loop
natural_expression: The most important thing during this first week back is arranging all the fridge magnets I collected along the way and picking out a few meaningful postcards to mail. Only then does the trip truly feel finished.

Both Zhang Wei and I prefer the timeless atmosphere of Gion in Kyoto. Compared to the hustle and bustle of Shibuya in Tokyo, this is where we can slowly wander the streets with our cameras, capturing those 'once-in-a-lifetime' cultural moments.

Seeing my parents laugh like children at Universal Studios Singapore told me I'd chosen the right place. It satisfied Mom's insistence on safety and hygiene, while letting Dad, a retired geography teacher, experience the joy of a modern theme park.

Ah Jie always reminisces about the hardcore hiking on New Zealand's Milford Track, but I prefer the 'comfortable' awe of Iceland's black sand beaches. Without rushing, just standing before the basalt columns, you can feel nature's overwhelming presence.

Standing before the Colosseum, my mind was filled with the ancient Roman history my father, Lin Jianguo, had told me about. Although he never visited, our video calls turned him into a 'history consultant' who breathed life into these ruins before my eyes.

For me, the Chiang Mai Night Market is a prime spot for street photography capturing local life. However, when I took my cousin Lin Yue there, she was only interested in checking off trendy dessert shops. This clash in pace made me realize that for long trips, I need to travel with like-minded companions.

While taking the cogwheel train from Interlaken up to Jungfraujoch, Zhang Wei saved the day by using Excel to calculate the best seats and timing for viewing the scenery. Without her, we would have missed the most breathtaking snowscapes; she is the perfect travel partner.

core_entity: George Town Street Art Lane, Penang
natural_expression: While exploring the street art lanes in George Town, Penang, I made a point to take my mom to try the roadside char kway teow. The lively atmosphere and familiar Chinese-speaking environment completely cured her anxiety about not finding food to her taste while abroad.

While photographing Botticelli's 'Primavera' at the Uffizi Gallery, I applied the low ISO technique Old K taught me. Although flash was prohibited, capturing the texture under natural light is the true essence of humanistic photography.

core_entity: Lantern Night in Hoi An Ancient Town
natural_expression: I remember the night we released lanterns in Hoi An Ancient Town; it was bustling around me, yet I felt an inexplicable loneliness. At that moment, I especially missed the taste of the pickled mustard greens my mom had tucked into my bag back home.

If Ah Jie hadn't dragged me to go skydiving in Queenstown, New Zealand, back then, I might never have known I could release stress at 10,000 meters. That was my most疯狂 outburst before transitioning from product operations to UX research.

In Montmartre, Paris, Zhang Wei and I didn't crowd into the Sacré-Cœur Basilica; instead, we found an unassuming little café and sat there all afternoon. This is what we define as 'city walking': just drinking coffee and watching passersby, skipping the tourist spots.

My first solo trip abroad was to Kyoto in 2018. When I walked through the thousands of torii gates at Fushimi Inari Taisha alone, the confidence I gained from handling all unexpected situations independently became the starting point for all my travel courage today.

travel_behavior: Book a short trip to Kamakura or Dali, choose a seaside homestay, leave the itinerary open, schedule only coffee drinking and listening to the waves, and refuse intensive sightseeing.
natural_expression: Recently, the pressure from the project launch was so intense I couldn't sleep. Without hesitation, I booked a ticket to Kamakura. This time, I won't queue for the Enoden train; I'll just zone out at a seaside homestay, drink coffee, and let the sound of the waves wash away my anxiety.

travel_behavior: Travel alone to museums or historical sites in Rome or Kyoto for a half-day of quiet observation, seeking career direction through dialogue with history
natural_expression: Whenever I hit a bottleneck in user research, I want to fly back to Rome and sit all afternoon in front of the Colosseum ruins. Reflecting on the history my father told me about brings a sense of depth that helps me see the path ahead clearly again.

emotion: Excitement/Celebration (promotion or completing a major project)
travel_behavior: Plan high-challenge activities like glacier hiking in Iceland or skydiving in New Zealand to release dopamine through extreme physical stimulation
natural_expression: Finally wrapped up that huge project! I have to reward myself with a glacier hike in Iceland, or go skydiving in New Zealand like I did with Ah Jie back in the day. If I don't go a little crazy, it wouldn't feel like a proper celebration of this joy.

travel_behavior: Visit night markets or local hole-in-the-wall eateries in Penang, Chengdu, or Changsha to find comfort in the lively atmosphere and familiar flavors
natural_expression: Working late alone in Hangzhou makes me miss home terribly. That's when I long for the night markets in George Town, Penang. The streets filled with Chinese chatter and the aroma of fried char kway teow instantly cure my loneliness.

travel_behavior: Face snow-capped mountains or the aurora in Interlaken or Reykjavik, letting the grandeur of nature dissolve the trivial worries of daily life
natural_expression: Standing beneath the Jungfrau in Interlaken, gazing at glaciers that are millions of years old, my anxiety over KPIs suddenly feels insignificant. Nature is truly the best therapist.

travel_behavior: Revisit Universal Studios Singapore or look at photos from past trips with parents to relive warm moments of family connection
natural_expression: I stumbled upon that family photo we took at Universal Studios Singapore. Seeing how happily Mom and Dad were smiling warms my heart. That trip taught me that companionship is the most enduring form of love.

travel_behavior: Reflecting on solo trips to Japan in 2018 or self-driving in Iceland in 2021 to reaffirm one's ability to solve problems independently.
natural_expression: Whenever I face a tough problem, I recall my 2018 experience in Kyoto where I missed my train but managed to resolve the situation alone. That 'I can handle anything' confidence is now my foundation for tackling workplace challenges.

travel_behavior: Recalling style conflicts with cousin Lin Yue in Thailand to reinforce future criteria for selecting travel companions and cherishing like-minded friendships.
natural_expression: Remembering how my cousin and I argued in Chiang Mai over whether to visit museums or check in at trendy spots makes me even more grateful for best friends like Zhang Wei. Travel isn't just about seeing scenery; it's a process of filtering for like-minded people.

travel_behavior: Starting to research routes for the Norwegian Fjords or Machu Picchu in Peru, conducting detailed preparatory planning for future Bucket List destinations.
natural_expression: Even though I can't go to Antarctica right now, just researching hiking trails and photography spots in the Norwegian Fjords fills me with anticipation for the future. This is my spiritual charging station.

travel_behavior: Feeling the greatness of human civilization when facing original artworks at the Uffizi Gallery in Florence or the Louvre in Paris, thereby letting go of self-centeredness.
natural_expression: The moment I stared at 'Primavera' in Florence, the beauty spanning hundreds of years gave me goosebumps. In the face of great art, personal gains and losses truly matter little.

travel_behavior: Opening a backpack packed by mom with cold medicine and pickled vegetables, or strolling through the safe and clean streets of Japan or Singapore, feeling the security of being loved.
natural_expression: Opening my backpack in a foreign land and feeling the pickled vegetables and cold medicine my mom insisted on packing gave me a sense of reassurance that felt even more comforting than staying in a five-star hotel.

travel_behavior: Self-driving along Iceland's Ring Road or running by Qinghai Lake, experiencing absolute freedom with no signal and no work messages.
natural_expression: I still remember those two days self-driving around Iceland in 2021. My phone had no signal, I didn't have to reply to DingTalk messages; there was only the sound of the wind and the engine. That sense of complete freedom made me decide to transfer to a role I truly love.

travel_activity: Strictly filter for 'design-focused boutique homestays' or 'conveniently located 3-star+ hotels' when booking accommodation.
companion_behavior: Firmly refuse noisy multi-bed dorms in hostels, but specifically check for lobbies with public social spaces to observe people.
context: When planning itineraries in Kyoto, Paris, or Chiang Mai, prioritize bedding quality and hygiene to ensure restful sleep that supports the next day's City Walk.
natural_expression: I have a strict rule for booking rooms: absolutely no multi-bed hostel dorms, but it must have great design or an excellent location. For instance, last time in Kyoto, I chose a renovated old Machiya homestay with a public living room. I could chat with travelers from various countries there at night and still get a good night's sleep during the day.

travel_activity: Always select a window seat for long-haul flights; prefer trains for travel within Europe or Japan.
companion_behavior: Enjoy the changing scenery along the way as part of the journey, and adhere to the principle of 'no night driving' when self-driving.
context: Utilize travel time for observation and rest when flying from Hangzhou to Reykjavik, or traveling from Interlaken, Switzerland to Florence.
natural_expression: Whenever I fly, I always grab a window seat; watching the clouds change is very healing. Once in Europe or Japan, I prefer trains. For example, taking the cogwheel train up the mountain from Interlaken, the scenery along the way is even more captivating than the destination. As for self-driving, I absolutely never drive at night; safety always comes first.

travel_activity: Use a Sony A7C II with a 20-70mm lens to capture street human-interest moments and grand landscapes.
companion_behavior: Insist on using natural light for photography, reject overly retouched portraits, and use a DJI Pocket 3 to record dynamic video.
context: When engaging in photography创作 in Hoi An Ancient Town during the lantern night, on the streets of Rome, or at Iceland's black sand beaches.
natural_expression: My standard setup is always the Sony A7C II paired with that 20-70mm lens, focusing on portability. When shooting lanterns in Hoi An or street photography in Rome, I only use natural light and never over-smooth skin tones. That authentic texture is the memory I want to preserve. I also use the Pocket 3 to capture some video footage.

travel_activity: Dive into local haunts like hole-in-the-wall eateries, morning markets, or night markets to find food.
companion_behavior: Firmly reject tourist group meals, but make it a point to explore 2-3 specialty coffee shops in every city, with deep knowledge of coffee and desserts.
context: When searching for Char Kway Teow in George Town, Penang, hunting for niche cafes in Montmartre, Paris, or browsing the weekend night market in Chiang Mai.
natural_expression: When eating out, I never go to restaurants that cater to tour groups; instead, I seek out the crowded hole-in-the-wall spots locals love, like the roadside Char Kway Teow stalls in Penang which taste the most authentic. However, I can't live without coffee. In every city I visit, such as Paris or Chiang Mai, I make it a non-negotiable ritual to explore two specialty coffee shops; that's my 'life-sustaining' ceremony.

travel_activity: Collect only one unique fridge magnet, a set of local postcards, and practical tableware or textile items from each city
companion_behavior: Firmly refuse to buy generic souvenirs mass-produced in Yiwu, and mail postcards to oneself or friends to complete the travel cycle
context: During the shopping phase before concluding trips to Florence, Singapore, or Queenstown
natural_expression: I'm very picky about what I buy; I don't even glance at those generic Yiwu-style trinkets found everywhere. I only collect fridge magnets unique to each city, along with practical tableware or textiles. Most importantly, I buy a few local postcards and mail them to myself or Zhang Wei after returning home; that's the only way a trip feels truly complete.

travel_activity: Discuss the historical background of the destination with Dad, Lin Jianguo, before departure to confirm the 'historical consultant's' advice
companion_behavior: Accept Mom, Chen Xiulan's, habit of stuffing the backpack with cold medicine and pickled mustard tubers before departure as a 'logistical support' talisman for peace of mind
context: During the family preparation stage before every trip to Europe (e.g., Rome, Paris) or long-haul international travel
natural_expression: Before every trip to Europe, I need to video chat with my dad, that 'geography teacher,' about local history to feel secure. Right before leaving, my mom will inevitably stuff my bag full of cold medicine and pickled mustard tubers. Although I think it's a bit excessive, touching those medicine packets gives me immense peace of mind when I'm in a foreign land.

travel_activity: Collaborate with best friend Zhang Wei: she handles the Excel itinerary and price comparisons, while I focus on uncovering hidden gems and taking photos
companion_behavior: Avoid long-distance, in-depth tours with travel companions whose rhythms don't match (such as a cousin who only cares about checking in at influencer spots), and insist on finding like-minded partners
context: Collaboration mode when planning complex, long-haul itineraries for regions like Kansai (Japan), Italy, or Switzerland
natural_expression: Zhang Wei and I make a perfect team; she creates Excel spreadsheets calculating every penny and minute, while I focus on finding those obscure photo spots no one knows about. Ever since that unpleasant disagreement with my cousin in Thailand due to our different paces, I'm even more convinced that for long trips, I should only travel with complementary peers like her.

travel_activity: Opt for a 'semi-independent travel' model: handle major transportation independently, then join small groups or customize locally based on circumstances
companion_behavior: Prefer walking or public transport within cities for deep 'City Walk' experiences, rejecting superficial bus tours that rush through sights
context: Specific sightseeing methods in places like Hoi An (Vietnam), Penang (Malaysia), or Paris (France)
natural_expression: I never sign up for those full-day bus tours. I always book my own flights for major transport and decide on the ground whether to join a small group or customize the experience. In cities like Paris or Penang, I prefer exploring on foot and using local buses or subways; it's the only way to truly integrate into the local rhythm of life.

travel_activity: Organize the month's photos on the last weekend of every month, color-grade and archive them, and select 9 to post on WeChat Moments
companion_behavior: Write concise travel blog posts documenting psychological shifts and cultural observations during the trip, rather than mere chronological logs
context: The review phase after returning from long-haul trips to places like Iceland, New Zealand, or anywhere else
natural_expression: Returning from a trip isn't the end. On the last weekend of every month, I import photos from my Sony camera and meticulously color-grade them in Lightroom. I select the nine most storytelling images for WeChat Moments and write a blog post reflecting on my feelings at the time—like the sense of solitude I felt while hiking in New Zealand. That's what makes a complete record.

travel_activity: When feeling lost, intentionally scheduling a 'silent period' to visit museums, churches, or historical sites
companion_behavior: Avoiding intensive interaction with companions; instead, engaging in inner dialogue while facing historical ruins alone to find direction in career or life
context: Specific behaviors exhibited in culturally rich locations such as the Colosseum in Rome, temples in Kyoto, or ancient sites in Xi'an
natural_expression: When I feel lost at work, I always reserve half a day for 'silent time' in my itinerary. For instance, in front of the Colosseum in Rome, I let Zhang Wei explore on her own while I sit alone, zoning out and reflecting on the history my father used to tell me. This dialogue with the past always helps me find a new direction.

travel_activity: When celebrating excitedly, proactively challenging high-difficulty activities (such as glacier hiking or skydiving) while setting safety boundaries
companion_behavior: Inspired by former colleague A-Jie to love nature, but aware of physical limitations, opting for 'comfort-style hiking' rather than purely hardcore outdoor adventures
context: Self-challenges during trips such as skydiving in Queenstown (New Zealand), glacier hiking in Iceland, or traveling across the western Sichuan plateau
natural_expression: When something great happens, I might go wild just like when A-Jie dragged me to go skydiving in Queenstown back in the day, or head to Iceland to walk on a glacier. But I know my limits; I won't attempt purely hardcore long-distance treks like the Milford Track. I prefer a 'comfort-style' sense of awe that releases energy while ensuring safety.

travel_activity: Regularly checking flight price fluctuations on Friday nights and following official accounts for special flight deals
companion_behavior: Making long-term budget plans and route rehearsals for Bucket List destinations (such as the Norwegian Fjords or Peru)
context: Preparing in advance for long-distance international trips planned for the next 1-2 years, from home in Hangzhou
natural_expression: Every Friday night is my 'dream planning time.' I keep an eye on flight prices, especially for routes from Hangzhou to the Norwegian Fjords or Peru. Although I can't go to Antarctica right now, doing advance budgeting and route research keeps me expecting and motivated for the future.

fragments: Recently, the project pressure has been so overwhelming that I just want to escape; my first instinct is to go zone out by the sea in Kamakura, just like last time in Kyoto.
Speaking of which, without Zhang Wei helping me plan the route during that trip to Kyoto, I wouldn't have been able to wander through Gion with such peace of mind to heal myself.
proactive_trigger: When the user mentions work stress or a desire to visit the Japanese seaside, associate this with their collaborative chemistry with Zhang Wei and their healing experience in Kyoto.

fragments: Sometimes when I feel lost at work, I recall the feeling of standing in front of the Colosseum, listening to my dad explain ancient Roman history via video call.

Although my old man never made it to Rome, he's now applying all that historical and geographical knowledge in his head to researching the timetable for the Qinghai-Tibet Railway trip we're planning for next year.

fragments: I must go skydiving in Queenstown, New Zealand to celebrate this promotion; after all, that's where Ah Jie first got me hooked.
But I've learned my lesson now. Although he influenced me to love the outdoors, I only do 'comfort-style hiking.' Let him handle the hardcore trails like the Milford Track.

fragments: When working overtime alone in Hangzhou and feeling especially homesick, I miss the char kway teow at the George Town night market in Penang; it's a flavor even my mom would accept.

Actually, no matter where I go, as long as I can feel the cold medicine and pickled vegetables my mom forcibly stuffed into my backpack, I feel the same sense of security as eating at a street stall in Penang.

fragments: Every Friday night, I check flight prices from Hangzhou to the Norwegian Fjords to see if there are any deals.

Actually, the main reason for going to Norway is to photograph the aurora. When the time comes, I'll have to ask Old K which obscure spots have less light pollution lately; he's extremely professional in that area.

After that trip, I realized that for long-distance travel, I need someone like Zhang Wei. She handles the itinerary while I take photos; clear division of labor prevents arguments.

At that moment, I missed home terribly, especially thinking about the pickled vegetables my mom forcibly stuffed into my bag; that was the only thing that could cure my loneliness.

If she hadn't calculated the timing and seating perfectly, we never would have secured the best viewing spots on the Jungfrau cogwheel train, and the snow scenery wouldn't have looked nearly as beautiful.

fragments: Looking back to 2018, being able to handle missing a train alone in Kyoto on my first solo trip gave me immense confidence.

fragments: This Singles' Day, I'm not buying clothes; I'm keeping my eyes on camera lenses, thinking I need to upgrade my gear for the next trip to Iceland to shoot the aurora.
After all, Old K mentioned before that to get great shots in Iceland's low-light conditions, the lens aperture and parameter settings are crucial, so I need to prepare in advance.

This is what we define as a 'City Walk': no sightseeing, just drinking coffee and watching passersby. This kind of pace can only be perfectly achieved when I'm with her.

fragments: Seeing my parents laugh like children at Universal Studios Singapore made me deeply realize that travel is also a bond that connects family affection.
Since then, I've made 'family-friendly' a mandatory criterion. So, no matter how busy I am next year, I will accompany my father to fulfill his wish of riding the Qinghai-Tibet Railway.
